items.headline: City Council reviews capital improvement projects for next fiscal year
items.summary: Mountain View is planning infrastructure and development investments for fiscal year 2026-27. This study session determines which projects get funding, potentially affecting roads, parks, transit, and other city amenities.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council is introducing an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect housing rights and responsibilities for renters and landlords in Sunnyvale. Details on specific protections being added or modified were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: City releases annual youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, along with annual reports on youth empowerment and intervention programs. These reports outline the city's investments in youth services and library improvements.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus floor area for rehabilitation
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status and granting an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This could enable a substantial expansion of the downtown building.

items.headline: Major rezoning proposed for San Antonio Road mixed-use development
items.summary: A proposal to rezone property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial to planned community zoning would allow an eight-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing density in that corridor.

items.headline: Mountain View postpones zoning decision for 333 Franklin Street
items.summary: The Planning Commission cancelled its April 8 Administrative Zoning hearing and rescheduled it to April 22, 2026. A development project at 333 Franklin Street that was set to be reviewed will now be heard at the later date pending re-notice to the public.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the available agenda information. Residents may want to follow up on what construction project this contract covers and where it will take place in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City gets grant for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts school
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will help expand cultural and educational programming at the South Bay landmark.
